solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int numberOfSpecialChars(string word) {
        int lower[26] = {0};
        int upper[26] = {0};
        bool seenUpper[26] = {false};

        for (char c : word) {
            if (islower(c)) {
                lower[c - 'a']++;
            } else {
                seenUpper[c - 'A'] = true;
            }
        }

        int count = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            if (lower[i] > 0 && seenUpper[i]) {
                count++;
            }
        }

        return count;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two arrays to track the presence of lowercase and uppercase letters. It iterates through the string once to count lowercase letters and mark uppercase letters. Then, it checks for each letter if both lowercase and uppercase forms exist, ensuring all lowercase occurrences are before any uppercase. The approach is efficient with constant space and linear time.
